about.p.4: Discover the profound meaning behind the Festival of Sacrifice and how it unites our beautiful Afghan diaspora.
about.p.5: Eid al-Adha, meaning "Feast of the Sacrifice," is the latter of the two official holidays celebrated within Islam. It honors the absolute dedication of Ibrahim (Abraham) to sacrifice his son as an act of obedience to God's command.
about.p.6: In Afghan culture, the focus is placed heavily on giving back. The meat from the sacrificed animal (Qurbani) is traditionally divided into three parts: one third is kept by the family, one third is given to relatives and friends, and the final third is given absolutely free to the poor and needy.
about.p.7: Beyond the religious rites, Eid al-Adha in the Afghan community is synonymous with expansive family gatherings centered around the Dastarkhwan (a traditional spread of food). Homes are opened to neighbors, and generations unite to tell stories, sip saffron tea, and share meals composed of Kabuli Pulao, Mantu, and an array of sweet delicacies like Sheer Khurma.
about.p.8: This bazaar seeks to replicate that warmth. It is a space where the diaspora can physically connect, share these timeless traditions, and allow our youth to fully experience the beauty of our heritage.

faq.p.9: Everything you need to know about the upcoming heritage celebration, vendor opportunities, and cultural experiences.
faq.p.10: The Afghan Eid Bazaar is held annually at the Heritage Pavilion Grounds. This year's celebration runs from June 14th to June 16th.
faq.p.11: Doors open daily from 10:00 AM until 9:00 PM. We recommend arriving early to experience the morning Sufi music sets.
faq.p.12: General admission is free for all attendees to ensure the cultural heritage remains accessible. However, some specialized workshop sessions in the Cultural Gallery and premium seating for the Evening Attan performances require a separate ticket purchase.
faq.p.13: We welcome artisans specializing in traditional Afghan crafts, textiles, and cuisine. Applications are processed through our curated portal.
faq.p.14: Kabuli Pulao, Mantu (dumplings), and various artisanal kebabs.
faq.p.15: Sheer Khurma, Firni with rosewater, and traditional saffron tea.
faq.p.16: Our heritage curators are available to answer specific questions regarding historical artifacts, family roots, and travel arrangements.
faq.p.17: © 2024 Afghan Eid Bazaar. A Heritage Curator Experience.

home.hero.title: Experience the Spirit of Afghan Heritage.
home.hero.desc: A curated gathering celebrating the resilience, craft, and communal joy of the Afghan diaspora. Join us for an unforgettable bazaar of artisanal treasures and cultural storytelling.
home.hero.btn_contact: Contact Organizer arrow_forward
home.spirit.title: The Essence of Sacrifice & Unity
home.spirit.desc: Eid Al-Adha is more than a celebration; it is a profound testament to faith, the act of giving, and the strength of our global community.
home.spirit.box1.title: Event Calendar
home.spirit.box1.desc: Live music, poetry readings, and calligraphy workshops scheduled across three days.
home.spirit.box3.title: Volunteer Drive
home.spirit.box3.desc: Lend your hands and heart to make this event a success for the entire community.
home.cta.title: Stay Connected with the Heritage
home.cta.desc: Receive exclusive invitations to cultural nights, early access to the bazaar marketplace, and stories from our artisan network.
footer.desc: Curating authentic experiences that bridge generations and geographies through the beauty of Afghan culture.

index.p.5: Honoring the devotion of Prophet Ibrahim, we reflect on the values of selflessness and spiritual commitment that anchor our heritage.
index.p.6: The Bazaar serves as a vibrant crossroads where families unite, elders share wisdom, and the youth reconnect with their ancestral roots.
index.p.7: In the spirit of Qurbani, we emphasize the importance of sharing our blessings with those in need, fostering a cycle of global compassion.
index.p.8: Discover rare silk scarves, hand-knotted carpets, and artisanal jewelry that tell stories of Kabul, Herat, and Mazar-i-Sharif.

sponsors.p.13: Supporting the journey of Afghan craftsmanship across continents, ensuring every piece of heritage reaches its new home safely.
sponsors.p.14: Dedicated to the preservation of traditional Afghan jewelry making and lapidary arts.
sponsors.p.15: Our guardians provide the essential funding for cultural workshops, live performances, and artisan training programs.
sponsors.p.16: Promoting contemporary Afghan fashion through global retail channels.
sponsors.p.17: Connecting culture seekers with the hidden gems of Central Asia.
sponsors.p.18: Sustainable sourcing of Afghanistan's legendary semi-precious stones.
sponsors.p.19: Purveyors of fine Afghan saffron and herbal tea blends.

team.p.9: Our dedicated team of cultural directors, ethnographers, and artisan liaisons working tirelessly to bring the authentic Afghan Eid Bazaar to life.
team.p.10: With over 15 years in cultural management, Zabiullah oversees the bazaar's vision, ensuring every stall reflects the true heart of Kabul and Herat.
team.p.11: Laila works directly with village weavers and jewelers across Afghanistan, bridging the gap between remote workshops and the global bazaar.
team.p.12: An ethnographer by trade, Dr. Shah ensures that our exhibition of Eid traditions remains historically accurate and respectful of regional nuances.
team.p.13: Mariam manages our dedicated force of 200+ volunteers, fostering a culture of hospitality and service that defines the Eid experience.
team.p.14: The mechanical mind of the bazaar, Omar ensures the complex movements of goods and infrastructure run seamlessly during the festive rush.
team.p.15: Sahar curates the aesthetic journey of the bazaar, hand-selecting every piece of lapis, silk, and saffron to meet our high standards.
team.p.16: Behind every successful exhibition is a chorus of selfless hands. To our 2024 volunteer drive: thank you for being the warmth in our hospitality and the strength in our traditions. You are the true curators of the community spirit.
team.button.26: View Roles arrow_forward
vendors.h2.2: Master Artisans Selected for Eid
vendors.p.11: Discover the souls behind the heritage. From the vibrant looms of Herat to the silver workshops of Kabul, we present Afghanistan's finest masters of craft.
vendors.p.12: "Every knot tells a thousand year story."
vendors.p.13: Intricate patterns passed down through seven generations of silk masters.
vendors.p.14: Lapis Lazuli and Silver smithing from the heart of the Hindu Kush.
vendors.p.15: Each vendor has been verified for cultural authenticity and fair-trade practices. Supporting these craftsmen ensures the survival of ancient Afghan techniques.
vendors.p.17: Specializing in double-knot silk rugs and traditional Herati floral embroidery patterns.
vendors.p.19: Direct-from-source Lapis Lazuli jewelry set in handcrafted sterling silver filigree.
vendors.p.21: World-renowned pomegranates, premium Grade A saffron, and organic dried fruits.
vendors.p.22: Subscribe to receive exclusive access to our Eid collection drops and stories from our master craftsmen.
vendors.p.23: A Heritage Curator Experience dedicated to preserving and promoting the profound artistry of Afghan craftsmanship to a global audience.
